 {"id":"bd-54qp","title":"Pie: Implement title rendering and custom slice colors","description":"# Pie Chart Rendering Improvements

## Gaps
- Title (\`pie title "Title"\`) is parsed but not rendered in SVG output
- Custom color per slice not supported (uses theme defaults)
- showData flag parsed but value display not implemented
- Legend box not rendered

## Implementation Plan
1. Render pie title as centered text above the chart
2. When showData is true, show percentage and value next to each slice label
3. Support custom colors via theme or init directive
4. Add legend box showing slice name → color mapping","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:46:07.810142830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-24T00:54:13.759642754Z","closed_at":"2026-03-24T00:54:13.759113101Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","pie","render"],"comments":[{"id":158,"issue_id":"bd-54qp","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:42Z"},{"id":177,"issue_id":"bd-54qp","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. pie title "Browser Usage" with 3 slices → verify title renders above chart as centered text
2. pie showData with slices → verify percentage AND value labels appear next to each slice
3. Pie with single slice → verify full circle renders (edge case)
4. Pie with 10+ slices → verify all slices visible, labels don't overlap
5. Slice with value 0 → verify zero-width slice handled gracefully (no crash, no visible wedge)
6. Theme-based colors → verify each slice gets a distinct color from the theme palette","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:41:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5k51","title":"EPIC: Reality Check Gap Closure","description":"## Purpose
Close every gap identified in the 2026-04-09 reality check audit. The audit compared README/AGENTS.md promises against actual code, tests, and deployment state. While the project is substantially honest (86k lines, all 24 diagram types render, all CLI commands work), several gaps threaten credibility:

1. **npm install claim is false**: @frankenmermaid/core returns 404 on npm registry. README tells users to run `npm install @frankenmermaid/core` which will fail.
2. **install.sh never committed**: README's `curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash` links to a file that doesn't exist on GitHub (untracked locally).
3. **classDef/style directives silently dropped**: Parsed but never applied to SVG render output. Users can write `classDef` and see no effect — no error, no warning, just silent failure.
4. **README understates FNX integration status**: Described as optional feature flag; actually the project's largest unfinished initiative (~25 open P1 beads).
5. **Long-tail diagram fidelity unproven**: All 16 tested types produce valid SVG, but visual quality (correct layout for sankey flows, pie slice angles, gitGraph branch lanes) has no golden-test coverage.
6. **Sequence advanced features incomplete**: Activation boxes, interaction fragments (alt/opt/loop), and notes are only partially parsed.
7. **Working tree hygiene**: Dozens of untracked audit files cluttering git status (fixed by .gitignore update in this session).

## Relationship to Existing Work
- FNX integration has its own epic (bd-ml2r); this epic does NOT duplicate that. Instead we create a single bead for README honesty about FNX status.
- Typography work (bd-3gq.2) is in progress; classDef styling is a SEPARATE concern (parsed→rendered wiring, not font metrics).
- "Write README" bead exists but covers feature comparison, not factual accuracy of install instructions.

## Success Criteria
- Every README install instruction either works or is corrected.
- classDef/style directives produce visible output in SVG or emit a diagnostic explaining they're unsupported.
- Golden tests added for at least 6 long-tail diagram types.
- Sequence advanced features either work or are honestly documented as unsupported.
- README FNX section accurately describes current state.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:23:14.879661635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T20:23:14.879661635Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Set recompute_duration_us to the computed duration and add a regression test ensuring the value is populated.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T18:11:58.229130125Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T18:19:14.544645637Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T18:19:14.544450998Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-5slz","title":"Replace hardcoded diagram title color in scene renderer","description":"## What
Replace hardcoded dark gray (#1f2937) for diagram titles in the scene-based render path (render_scene_document_with_ir) with a theme-aware value.

## Why
The scene-based renderer at fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs line ~341 uses `.fill("#1f2937")` for diagram titles. This dark color is invisible on Dark, Blueprint, and Neon themes.

## Technical Context
This is in `render_scene_document_with_ir()` where the title is rendered BEFORE `let theme = resolve_theme()` at line ~361. The theme is not yet available when the title is rendered.

## Options
1. **Move title rendering after theme resolution** — requires restructuring the function, since the title needs to be in the SVG before the main content for z-ordering
2. **Use CSS custom property** — `.fill("var(--fm-text-color, #1f2937)")` — works when theme CSS is embedded
3. **Pre-resolve theme for title** — call `resolve_theme()` earlier just for the title color

Option 2 is the simplest and most robust since the theme CSS block IS embedded before the title element.

## Specific Changes
- fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs line ~341: Change `.fill("#1f2937")` to `.fill("var(--fm-text-color, #1f2937)")`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Diagram titles are readable on all 10 theme presets
- No visual regression on Default theme (fallback is the same dark gray)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:32.780097774Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:13.503408799Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:13.502905336Z","close_reason":"Title now uses var(--fm-text-color, #1f2937) CSS custom property. Theme CSS defines --fm-text-color for all presets.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-65l0","title":"Wire classDef/style directives through to SVG render output","description":"## Problem
Mermaid classDef and style directives are PARSED by fm-parser (confirmed: the parser extracts class definitions and style assignments into the IR) but they are NEVER APPLIED during SVG rendering. This means:
- A user writes: `classDef important fill:#f9f,stroke:#333,stroke-width:4px`
- The parser stores this in the IR without error
- The SVG renderer completely ignores it
- The user sees default styling with NO warning or diagnostic

This is a silent failure — worse than a "not supported" error because the user doesn't know their styling is being dropped.

## Scope
1. **Survey the IR**: Determine where classDef data lives in MermaidDiagramIr after parsing. Check IrNode.classes, any classDef-specific structures in meta, and style directive handling.
2. **SVG render integration**: In fm-render-svg, when rendering a node, check if it has classes that match any classDef. If so, apply the CSS properties (fill, stroke, stroke-width, color, font-size, etc.) to the SVG element's inline style or as CSS custom properties.
3. **CSS generation**: Generate <style> rules in the SVG <defs> section for each classDef, scoped to the appropriate CSS selectors (e.g., `.node.important { fill: #f9f; }`).
4. **Style directive handling**: Wire the `style` directive (which applies inline styles to specific nodes) through to SVG output.
5. **Theme interaction**: Define precedence: theme defaults < classDef rules < inline style directives < init directive overrides. Document this.
6. **Diagnostic for partial support**: If any CSS property in a classDef is not supported by the SVG renderer, emit a Warning-level diagnostic listing the unsupported properties.

## Why This Matters
Styling is one of the most-used Mermaid features. Users migrating from mermaid-js will expect classDef to work. Silent failure erodes trust more than explicit "unsupported" errors.

## Dependencies
- fm-parser already parses classDef (verified).
- fm-core IR must carry the parsed data (verify exact fields).
- fm-render-svg must consume and apply it.

## Testing
- Unit test: classDef with fill/stroke/stroke-width produces matching SVG attributes.
- Unit test: style directive on specific node overrides classDef.
- Golden test: `flowchart_with_classdef.mmd` added to golden suite.
- Negative test: unsupported CSS property emits diagnostic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `classDef` definitions in Mermaid input produce visible style changes in SVG output.
- `style` directives on individual nodes produce visible inline styles.
- Unsupported CSS properties emit a warning diagnostic.
- Golden test captures the styled output for regression safety.
- README Limitations section updated to remove classDef from the "not yet applied" list.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:24:07.120164265Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T21:16:04.987246559Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-65l0","depends_on_id":"bd-5k51","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:04.067736729Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-65l0","depends_on_id":"bd-uwtl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:25:32.222608316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-68e5","title":"Timeline: Implement era backgrounds and time-scale formatting","description":"# Timeline Rendering Improvements

## Gaps
- Era/epoch background highlighting (sections should have colored background bands)
- Time-scale awareness (dates vs years vs arbitrary periods)
- Timeline axis/spine line connecting periods
- Event styling (different visual for different event types)
- Title rendering

## Implementation Plan
1. Draw section backgrounds as colored horizontal bands behind events
2. Add a horizontal timeline spine/axis line connecting period markers
3. Render title as centered header text
4. Apply visual distinction for different event categories if present","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:46:08.324800929Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T03:20:42.949853361Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T03:20:42.949583640Z","close_reason":"Added timeline-period and timeline-event CSS classes for visual distinction.
